title: Distortion mitigation during sintering of ceramics through the incorporation of ceramic precursor solutions
abstract: A method according to this application includes binder jet printing of a ceramic powder to form a body; applying a precursor solution to said body; and sintering said body, wherein said application of the precursor solution to the body mitigates distortion of the body during or after the sintering step. A ceramic powder body comprising: a ceramic powder; and a precursor decomposed from a precursor solution, the precursor having a rigid nanoparticle form and coated around the ceramic powder.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed: 1. A method comprising: binder jet printing of a ceramic powder to form a ceramic body; applying a precursor solution to said body; and sintering said body, wherein said application of the precursor solution to the body mitigates distortion of the body during or after the sintering step. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the ceramic power is combined with a binder during the binder jet printing of the body. 3. The method of claim 2, wherein the ceramic body is pre- sintered before applying the precursor solution. 4. The method of claim 1, wherein the body comprising the applied precursor solution is subjected to a decomposition reaction at a temperature that ranges from 200Â° C to 600Â° C, before the sintering. 5. The method claim 4, wherein the decomposition of the precursor solution yields a ceramic nano-particulate. 6. The method of claim 1, wherein the ceramic powder is one or more selected from the group consisting of titanium dioxide, silicon carbide, zirconia, and aluminum oxide, or a mixture thereof. 7. The method of claim 1, wherein the sintering of said body is conducted at a temperature that ranges from 1000Â° C to 1500Â° C. 8. The method of claim 1, wherein the precursor solution comprises at least one selected from the group consisting of metal alkoxides, metallic salt solutions, sol-gels, polymer-derived ceramics, and co-precipitation/calcination solutions. 9. The method of claim 1, wherein the applying comprises at least one selected from the group consisting of: applying the precursor solution layer by layer in conjunction with 

 the binder jet printing, immersing the body in the precursor solution, and infiltrating the body with the precursor solution. 10. The method of claim 1, wherein the sintering step comprises at least one selected from the group consisting of thermal treatment, chemical treatment, and UV treatment of the body. 11. The method of claim 1, wherein the ceramic powder comprises Ti0 2. 12. The method of claim 8, wherein the precursor solution comprises titanium (IV) bis(ammoniumlactato)dihydroxide. 13. A ceramic powder body comprising: a ceramic powder; and a precursor decomposed from a precursor solution, the precursor having a rigid nanoparticle form and coated around the ceramic powder. 14. The ceramic powder body of claim 13, wherein the ceramic powder body is formed by binder jet printing. 15. The ceramic powder body of claim 13, wherein the ceramic powder body comprises a ceramic powder that is one or more selected from the group consisting of titanium dioxide, silicon carbide, zirconia, and aluminum oxide, or a mixture thereof. 16. The ceramic powder body of claim 13, wherein the ceramic powder body further comprises a binder. 17. The ceramic powder body of claim 13, wherein the precursor solution comprises at least one selected from the group consisting of alkoxides, sol-gels, polymer-derived ceramics, and a co-precipitation/calcination solution. 18. The ceramic powder body of claim 13, wherein the average relative density of the sintered body is from 50 to 60%. 19. The ceramic powder body of claim 13, wherein the precursor solution is decomposed to yield a ceramic nanoparticle coating of the same ceramic material of the ceramic powder. 20. A method for preparing a positive mold for directionally solidified components comprising: binder jet printing a ceramic powder to form a ceramic body; applying a precursor solution to said body; and sintering said body, wherein said application of the precursor solution to the body mitigates distortion of the body during or after the sintering step.
